Summarize this long architectural design specification in exactly two bullet points: Distributed consensus algorithms like Raft and Paxos ensure data consistency across multiple replicated server nodes in cloud environments. When a leader node fails or experiences network partitions, follower nodes initiate leader elections using randomized election timeouts and heartbeat messages. State machines on all surviving nodes apply log entries sequentially once the majority quorum confirms replication. High throughput systems also implement read-write splitting, sharding across consistent hashing rings, and asynchronous snapshotting to prevent disk exhaustion. Despite these optimizations, cross-datacenter latency remains a fundamental physical bottleneck under CAP theorem tradeoffs.
